2605.24869.title: Lngram: N-gram Conditional Memory in Latent Space
2605.24869.paper_summary: Sequence modeling requires both compositional reasoning and local static knowledge retrieval, yet standard Transformers handle both through dense computation. Engram partially decouples retrieval from the backbone, but its token-based keys remain tied to text tokenization and hash compression. We propose Lngram, a latent-space conditional memory module that learns discrete symbols directly from hidden states and performs N-gram lookup over these symbols. This design removes the dependence on tokenizer IDs and naturally extends to non-text modalities. In our evaluated settings, Lngram outperforms Transformer and Engram baselines, consistently reduces perplexity in long-context language modeling, and effectively injects domain knowledge when added post hoc to pretrained models. Joint training with the backbone further surpasses full fine-tuning, while experiments on vision-language and vision-language-action tasks show overall gains. Analyses with LogitLens and CKA suggest that Lngram enables prediction-relevant information to emerge earlier, increasing effective depth with limited inference and memory overhead. Code is available at https://github.com/zyaaa-ux/Lngram.
2605.24869.paper_summary_zh: 序列建模需要組合推理和局部靜態知識檢索，但標準的Transformer通過密集計算處理這兩者。Engram 部分地將檢索與主幹解耦，但其基於標記的鍵仍然與文本標記化和哈希壓縮相關聯。我們提出了 Lngram，一種潛在空間條件記憶模塊，能直接從隱藏狀態學習離散符號，並對這些符號執行 N-gram 查找。這一設計消除了對標記器 ID 的依賴，並自然擴展到非文本模態。在我們評估的設置中，Lngram 超越了Transformer和 Engram 基準，持續降低長上下文語言建模中的困惑度，並在預訓練模型後添加時有效地注入領域知識。與主幹的聯合訓練進一步超越了完全微調，而在視覺-語言和視覺-語言-行動任務上的實驗顯示出整體增益。使用 LogitLens 和 CKA 的分析表明，Lngram 使得與預測相關的信息能夠更早出現，增加了有效深度，同時限制了推理和記憶的開銷。代碼可在 https://github.com/zyaaa-ux/Lngram 獲得。
2605.24869.author: Yunao Zheng et.al.
2605.24869.authors: Yunao Zheng, Guoyang Xia, Xiaojie Wang, Lei Ren
